markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Taw Launcher](/Weapons.aspx?ID=658)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conrasu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=343" />
<trait label="Deadly d10" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=174" />
<trait label="Modular (B, P, or S)" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=271" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 31

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 10 gp

**Damage** 1d10 Modular

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 2

**Range** 100 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

</column>

---

This complex device is a crossbow and fires small wooden bullets known as taws. A system of blades within the launcher can rapidly reshape a taw as it’s loaded, allowing the launcher to fire taws of different shapes, such as fléchettes.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-3" />
name: Taw Launcher

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bow Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=659)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Combination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=417" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 31

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 8 gp

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Hands** 1+

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

The bow staff is a whipstaff with a retracting spool of wire inside a metal cap on one end and a hooked protrusion on the other. A wielder trained in the weapon’s use can quickly spool and attach or detach the wire to transition the weapon between bow and staff functionality.

<title level="2">Melee</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Finesse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=179" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=112" />
<trait label="Parry" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=190" />
<trait label="Sweep" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=194" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d6 B

**Group** [Club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

<title level="2">Ranged</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Deadly D8" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=174" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=112" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d6 P

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 80 ft.

**Reload** 0
</row>

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-3" />
name: Bow Staff (Ranged)

>
[Crescent Cross](/Weapons.aspx?ID=660)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Combination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=417" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 31

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 4 gp

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Hands** 1

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

A crescent cross combines a small scizore with an arm-mounted crossbow apparatus that can hold up to three bolts at a time.

<title level="2">Melee</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Critical Fusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=402" />
<trait label="Parry" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=190" />
</traits>

**Damage** 1d4 S

**Group** [Knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9)

</column>

<title level="2">Ranged</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Capacity 3" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=399" />
<trait label="Parry" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=190" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d6 P

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-3" />

[Lancer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=661)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 8 gp

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Hands** 2

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

This lance has a heavy grip with two parallel crossbow fixtures built into it, making it a useful weapon for combats who prefer to keep their distance at all times.

<title level="2">Melee</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Critical Fusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=402" />

</column>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 ft.

**Reload** 2
</row>

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

[Mikazuki](/Weapons.aspx?ID=662)
</title>

<traits>

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Hands** 1+

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

The mikazuki combines a sansetsukon with a thin length of metal string and several locking mechanisms built into the joints, allowing it to be quickly locked into configuration as a bow.

<trait label="Backswing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=173" />

You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wrecker](/Weapons.aspx?ID=663)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Combination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=417" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 31

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 8 gp

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Hands** 2

**Category** Advanced

</column>

---

The wrecker combines a dwarven dorndergar with a heavy, gear-reinforced arm cover that allows it to be fired like an oversized sling, then retrieved and reloaded by manually activating a clockwork spool. A wrecker must be loaded to be switched from its ranged configuration to its melee configuration.

<title level="2">Melee</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Razing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=488" />
<trait label="Reach" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=192" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d8 B

**Group** [Flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7)

</column>

<title level="2">Ranged</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Ranged Trip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=251" />
<trait label="Razing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=488" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d6 B

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 20 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

**Group** [Sling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-13" />
name: Wrecker (Ranged)
